problem: Melinda will roll two standard six-sided dice and make a two-digit number with the two numbers she rolls. For example, if she rolls a 6 and a 3, she can either form 36 or 63. What is the probability that she will be able to make an integer between 10 and 20, inclusive? Express your answer as a common fraction.

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the scenario and what Melinda needs to do to form a two-digit number?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What are the possible outcomes when rolling two standard six-sided dice?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many outcomes would result in forming a two-digit number between 10 and 20 inclusive?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability of neither of the dice landing on a 1?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we find the probability of at least one die landing on a 1?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the probability, expressed as a common fraction, that Melinda will be able to make an integer between 10 and 20?
classified_questions.label: b
